notes: Adjusts June weekend rides because the athlete is at the cottage every weekend and returns to Montreal on Sundays, making three-hour Sunday rides unrealistic. Keeps Sundays as travel/recovery breaks and preserves durability with Friday volume where useful plus Saturday two-hour cottage endurance anchors. This week is bike-only: remove the Tuesday gym session while soreness from reintroducing gym settles.

workouts.title: Tuesday Easy Endurance
workouts.objective: Replace the removed gym session with low-cost aerobic work while soreness from reintroducing gym settles.
workouts.why_now: This keeps bike frequency high during a bike-only week without adding strength soreness or compromising Friday's bigger sustained-power ride.

targets.notes: 75-minute easy endurance ride at 55-68% FTP. No tempo. Optional low-force cadence only if legs feel normal.

steps.notes: Fast legs, low force. Skip if legs are sore.
Easy endurance extension. Keep pressure light.

nutrition.pre: Normal meal is enough. If hungry, add 20-30g carbs 30-60 min before.
nutrition.during: Water to thirst, roughly 400-750ml/hour if warm or sweating. No ride carbs needed unless starting hungry.
nutrition.post: Normal meal within one to two hours with 25-35g protein.
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: none required. Daily baseline only if already part of routine.

recovery.post_session: Normal meal and fluids. No extra lower-body work.
recovery.evening: Protect sleep; the week gets harder Friday.
recovery.next_morning_check: Wednesday proceeds only if legs feel normal and soreness is not increasing.

skill_focus.action: Keep cadence light and low force.
skill_focus.why: This adds coordination without loading sore gym muscles.
Easy frequency supports the build only if Friday still looks attractive.

mental_focus.intention: Use the bike to loosen up, not prove fitness.
mental_focus.why: The useful aggressive move this week is more bike rhythm with controlled cost, then a bigger Friday anchor.
mental_focus.reset: If you start pushing tempo, shift easier for 2 minutes and return to the endurance cap.

pre_interval_cues: Skip cadence drills if legs are sore.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Ride low endurance only and cut to 60 minutes if soreness increases.
execution_notes.success_criteria: You finish looser and still want to ride Wednesday or Friday.

workouts.title: Friday Long Tempo Durability
workouts.objective: Use the bigger Friday window for the week's main sustained-power stimulus while keeping Sunday free for travel and recovery.
workouts.why_now: Sunday return-to-Montreal logistics make a Sunday ride fragile this week, and Friday has more time available. Moving load to Friday preserves FTP-focused work without forcing the weekend.

targets.notes: Two-hour outdoor ride with 2 x 25 minutes tempo at 80-85% FTP. This is durable pressure, not threshold.

steps.notes: Tempo pressure. Smooth, repeatable, no threshold creep.

nutrition.pre: Carb-forward meal two to four hours before with about 1-2g/kg carbs. Add 20-30g carbs 30-60 min before if starting hungry.
nutrition.during: Use 40-70g carbs/hour and water to thirst. Start during the first 30 minutes.
nutrition.post: 25-35g protein plus 60-100g carbs across the next meal window.
nutrition.centr_recipe: High-protein Lentil Braise with Flatbread, Centr collection link: https://centr.com/blog/show/32824/10-high-protein-recipes-for-easy-meal-prep
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: electrolytes/sodium if hot or heavy sweating; otherwise none required. Daily baseline only if already part of routine.

recovery.post_session: Carbs plus protein within one to two hours, then normal dinner.
recovery.evening: No extra lower-body work. Saturday stays endurance only.
recovery.next_morning_check: Saturday endurance proceeds only if legs are normal and Friday did not feel forced.

skill_focus.action: Cap tempo at 85% FTP even if it feels easy.
skill_focus.why: The goal is durable sustained pressure that leaves Saturday available, not another threshold proof.

skill_focus.action: Rebuild power gradually after stops and corners.
skill_focus.why: Smooth outdoor execution helps make higher FTP usable without wasting energy in surges.

mental_focus.intention: Put the week's ambition in the right place.
mental_focus.why: A bigger Friday ride and a Sunday break fits the actual cottage weekend better than pretending Sunday has a clean training window.
mental_focus.reset: If power drifts above tempo, shift easier for 60 seconds, relax hands and jaw, and settle back under the cap.

pre_interval_cues: Start only if legs feel normal.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Hold the cap and finish clean. Do not lift above 85% FTP.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Convert the second tempo block to endurance at 58-70% FTP.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Both tempo blocks steady with no late fade, and Saturday still looks doable.

workouts.title: Friday Aerobic Durability Anchor
workouts.objective: Move the cottage-weekend endurance load into Friday so Saturday is not mandatory when bike or gym access at the cottage is unavailable.
workouts.why_now: The previous version assumed Saturday cottage bike access. That is not reliable. Friday is the dependable bike window, so this becomes the aerobic durability anchor after Thursday's threshold work.

source_context.week_objective: Build absorption with cottage access constraint

targets.notes: 2h15 endurance durability at 58-72% FTP. No tempo finish. This is aerobic volume after Thursday threshold, not another quality session.

steps.notes: Endurance durability. Fuel early, cap climbs, stay aerobic.

nutrition.pre: Carb-forward meal two to four hours before with about 1-2g carbs/kg. Add 20-30g carbs 30-60 min before if starting hungry.
nutrition.during: Start in the first 30 minutes. Target 40-60g carbs/hour and drink to thirst.
nutrition.post: 25-35g protein plus 60-90g carbs across the next meal window.
nutrition.centr_recipe: High-protein Lentil Braise with Flatbread, Centr collection link: https://centr.com/blog/show/32824/10-high-protein-recipes-for-easy-meal-prep
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: electrolytes/sodium if hot or heavy sweating; otherwise none required. Daily baseline only if already part of routine.

recovery.post_session: Carbs plus protein, fluids to thirst, and easy walking if legs stiffen.
recovery.evening: No extra lower-body work. Saturday is not mandatory unless bike access and weather are actually good.
recovery.next_morning_check: Saturday is rest, cottage work, or optional easy movement unless bike access is confirmed and legs are normal.

skill_focus.action: Cap climbs and avoid low-cadence grinding.
skill_focus.why: This keeps the Friday anchor aerobic after Thursday threshold instead of turning it into a second hard session.

skill_focus.action: Relax shoulders and jaw every 20 minutes.
skill_focus.why: Lower tension improves repeatable outdoor endurance and reduces wasted energy on longer rides.

mental_focus.intention: Make Friday the durable aerobic anchor, not another threshold test.
mental_focus.why: A real Friday endurance block preserves the weekly training dose when cottage bike access is uncertain.
mental_focus.reset: If you start pushing tempo, shift easier for two minutes and settle back under the endurance cap.

pre_interval_cues: This is the weekend bike anchor.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Correct. Extend only to 2h30 if daylight, logistics, and legs are all good.
execution_notes.success_criteria: You finish with steady power, no depletion, and no need to force Saturday.

workouts.title: Cottage Saturday Endurance
workouts.objective: Build weekend durability while keeping Sunday free for travel and recovery.
workouts.why_now: The week now uses Friday as the bigger load anchor and Sunday as a break, so Saturday should be steady endurance, not a two-day setup.

targets.notes: Two-hour endurance ride at 58-70% FTP. No tempo finish. Fuel from the first 30 minutes.

steps.notes: Endurance. Keep climbs capped and seated when practical.

nutrition.pre: Carb-forward meal two to four hours before with about 1-2g carbs/kg.
nutrition.during: Start in the first 30 min. Target 40-60g carbs/hour and drink to thirst.
nutrition.post: 25-35g protein plus 60-90g carbs across the next meal window.
nutrition.centr_recipe: Creamy Roasted Tofu & Cauliflower Korma, Centr collection link: https://centr.com/blog/show/32824/10-high-protein-recipes-for-easy-meal-prep
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: electrolytes/sodium if hot or heavy sweating; otherwise none required. Daily baseline only if already part of routine.

recovery.post_session: Carbs plus protein, fluids to thirst, and 5-10 min easy walking if legs tighten.
recovery.evening: No extra training. Let Sunday be a real break unless life opens an easy optional window.
recovery.next_morning_check: Sunday is rest/travel by default. Ride only if unusually fresh and logistics are calm.

skill_focus.action: Cap climbs and avoid torque spikes.
skill_focus.why: The goal is durable endurance without creating fatigue that follows you into Monday.

skill_focus.action: Check shoulders, jaw, and grip every 20-30 minutes.
skill_focus.why: Relaxed upper body saves energy over longer cottage roads.

mental_focus.why: Sunday recovery makes the Friday/Saturday load more repeatable across cottage weekends.
mental_focus.reset: If you start chasing speed, eat or drink if due, relax grip, and ride below the endurance cap for two minutes.

execution_notes.if_too_easy: Do not add tempo. Let the week end clean.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Finish steady, fueled, and not depleted.

workouts.title: Cottage Saturday Endurance
workouts.objective: Anchor the cottage weekend with one steady Saturday endurance ride and keep Sunday free for return logistics.
workouts.why_now: Sunday travel makes Sunday training fragile; Saturday is the reliable cottage endurance slot.

targets.notes: Two-hour endurance ride at 58-70% FTP. No tempo finish.

steps.notes: Endurance. Fuel early and keep climbs capped.

workouts.title: Cottage Saturday Endurance
workouts.objective: Use Saturday as the cottage endurance anchor while Sunday remains travel/recovery.
workouts.why_now: This preserves durability without pretending Sunday has a clean long-ride window.

targets.notes: Two-hour endurance ride at 58-70% FTP. No tempo finish.

steps.notes: Endurance. Fuel early and cap climbs.

workouts.title: Cottage Saturday Endurance
workouts.objective: Complete the June overload week with a practical Saturday endurance anchor.
workouts.why_now: Sunday is kept clear for return logistics, so Saturday carries the weekend endurance dose.

targets.notes: Two-hour endurance ride at 58-70% FTP. No tempo finish.

steps.notes: Endurance. Fuel early and cap climbs.
